tagline: Calculate Claude API costs by model, tokens, and usage
keywords: Claude API cost, Claude pricing, Anthropic API cost, Claude tokens cost, Claude Opus price
calcFn: var pricing={haiku:{input:0.25,output:1.25},sonnet:{input:3,output:15},opus:{input:15,output:75}}; var p=pricing[model]; var inCost=(inputTokens/1000000)*p.input; var outCost=(outputTokens/1000000)*p.output; var costPerReq=inCost+outCost; var dailyCost=costPerReq*requests; var monthlyCost=dailyCost*days; var monthlyIn=inCost*requests*days; var monthlyOut=outCost*requests*days; return {costPerReq:'$'+fmt(costPerReq,6), dailyCost:dollar(dailyCost), monthlyCost:dollar(monthlyCost), inputCost:dollar(monthlyIn), outputCost:dollar(monthlyOut)};

faq.question: How much does the Claude API cost?
faq.answer: Claude Haiku costs $0.25/$1.25 per million input/output tokens. Sonnet costs $3/$15. Opus costs $15/$75. Most applications use Sonnet for the best balance of quality and cost.

faq.question: What is a token?
faq.answer: A token is roughly 3/4 of a word. 1,000 tokens is about 750 words. A typical conversation turn might use 500-2,000 input tokens and 200-1,000 output tokens.

faq.question: How do I reduce Claude API costs?
faq.answer: Use Haiku for simple tasks, Sonnet for most tasks, and Opus only for complex reasoning. Implement prompt caching, batch API for non-realtime work, and optimize prompt length.

formulas: Cost = (Input Tokens × Input Price/MTok) + (Output Tokens × Output Price/MTok)

tagline: Calculate GPT-4o and OpenAI API costs
keywords: OpenAI API cost, GPT-4 pricing, ChatGPT API cost, OpenAI tokens
calcFn: var pricing={gpt4omini:{input:0.15,output:0.60},gpt4o:{input:2.50,output:10},o1:{input:15,output:60},o3mini:{input:1.10,output:4.40}}; var p=pricing[model]; var inCost=(inputTokens/1000000)*p.input; var outCost=(outputTokens/1000000)*p.output; var costPerReq=inCost+outCost; var dailyCost=costPerReq*requests; var monthlyCost=dailyCost*days; return {costPerReq:'$'+fmt(costPerReq,6), dailyCost:dollar(dailyCost), monthlyCost:dollar(monthlyCost)};

faq.question: How much does GPT-4o cost?
faq.answer: GPT-4o costs $2.50 per million input tokens and $10 per million output tokens. GPT-4o Mini is significantly cheaper at $0.15/$0.60 per million tokens.

faq.question: GPT-4o vs Claude Sonnet which is cheaper?
faq.answer: GPT-4o at $2.50/$10 per MTok is slightly cheaper than Claude Sonnet at $3/$15 for input but more expensive for output. Total cost depends on your input/output ratio.

formulas: Monthly Cost = (Input Cost + Output Cost) × Requests × Days

tagline: Estimate token count from text length
keywords: token counter, LLM tokens, word to token converter, GPT token counter, Claude token counter
calcFn: var ratios={avg:1.33,code:1.5,simple:1.2}; var ratio=ratios[method]||1.33; var tokens=Math.round(words*ratio); var characters=Math.round(words*5); var pages=fmt(words/250,1); var costHaiku=(tokens/1000000)*0.25; var costSonnet=(tokens/1000000)*3; return {tokens:fmt(tokens,0)+' tokens', characters:fmt(characters,0), pages:pages, costHaiku:'$'+fmt(costHaiku,6), costSonnet:'$'+fmt(costSonnet,6)};

faq.question: How many tokens is 1000 words?
faq.answer: Approximately 1,330 tokens for English text. Code tends to use more tokens (about 1,500 per 1,000 words) due to special characters and syntax.

faq.question: How do tokens work in LLMs?
faq.answer: LLMs break text into tokens — subword pieces that may be whole words, word parts, or individual characters. Common words are single tokens, while rare words get split into multiple tokens.

tagline: Estimate monthly Claude Code usage costs for development
keywords: Claude Code cost, Claude Code pricing, Claude Code monthly cost, AI coding assistant cost
calcFn: var pricing={haiku:{input:0.25,output:1.25},sonnet:{input:3,output:15},opus:{input:15,output:75}}; var p=pricing[model]; var monthlyPrompts=hoursPerDay*daysPerMonth*promptsPerHour; var totalIn=monthlyPrompts*avgInputTokens; var totalOut=monthlyPrompts*avgOutputTokens; var inCost=(totalIn/1000000)*p.input; var outCost=(totalOut/1000000)*p.output; var monthlyCost=inCost+outCost; var costPerHour=monthlyCost/(hoursPerDay*daysPerMonth); var costPerPrompt=monthlyCost/monthlyPrompts; return {monthlyPrompts:fmt(monthlyPrompts,0), monthlyTokens:fmt((totalIn+totalOut)/1000000,1)+'M', monthlyCost:dollar(monthlyCost), costPerHour:dollar(costPerHour), costPerPrompt:'$'+fmt(costPerPrompt,4)};

faq.question: How much does Claude Code cost per month?
faq.answer: It depends on usage intensity. A typical developer using Sonnet with 10 prompts/hour for 6 hours/day might spend $50-150/month. Heavy Opus usage can be $500+/month.

faq.question: How do I reduce Claude Code costs?
faq.answer: Use Sonnet as your default model and switch to Opus only for complex tasks. Write clear, specific prompts to reduce back-and-forth. Use /compact to reduce context window size.

formulas: Monthly Cost = Monthly Prompts × (Input Tokens × Input Rate + Output Tokens × Output Rate)

tagline: Compare costs across Claude, GPT-4, Gemini, and other LLMs
keywords: LLM comparison, AI model comparison, Claude vs GPT-4, LLM pricing comparison
calcFn: var models=[{name:'Claude Haiku',i:0.25,o:1.25},{name:'Claude Sonnet',i:3,o:15},{name:'Claude Opus',i:15,o:75},{name:'GPT-4o Mini',i:0.15,o:0.60},{name:'GPT-4o',i:2.50,o:10}]; var costs=models.map(function(m){return {name:m.name,cost:inputTokens*m.i+outputTokens*m.o};}); costs.sort(function(a,b){return a.cost-b.cost;}); return {haikuCost:dollar(costs.find(function(c){return c.name==='Claude Haiku';}).cost), sonnetCost:dollar(costs.find(function(c){return c.name==='Claude Sonnet';}).cost), opusCost:dollar(costs.find(function(c){return c.name==='Claude Opus';}).cost), gpt4oMiniCost:dollar(costs.find(function(c){return c.name==='GPT-4o Mini';}).cost), gpt4oCost:dollar(costs.find(function(c){return c.name==='GPT-4o';}).cost), cheapest:costs[0].name+' at '+dollar(costs[0].cost)};

faq.question: Which LLM is cheapest?
faq.answer: For most use cases, GPT-4o Mini and Claude Haiku are the cheapest options. The best choice depends on whether you need speed, quality, or cost optimization.

faq.question: Claude Sonnet vs GPT-4o which is better value?
faq.answer: They are similarly priced. Claude Sonnet excels at coding and analysis while GPT-4o is strong at general tasks. Both cost roughly $3/$10-15 per million tokens.

formulas: Monthly Cost = Input Tokens (M) × Input Rate + Output Tokens (M) × Output Rate

tagline: Calculate costs for DALL-E, Midjourney, and Stable Diffusion
keywords: AI image cost, DALL-E pricing, Midjourney cost, AI art cost calculator
calcFn: var totalImages=imagesPerDay*days; var costPerImg={dalle3:0.04,dalle3hd:0.08,midjourney:10/200,sdapi:0.02}; var cost=costPerImg[service]||0.04; var monthlyCost; if(service==='midjourney'){monthlyCost=totalImages<=200?10:10+((totalImages-200)*0.05);cost=monthlyCost/totalImages;}else{monthlyCost=totalImages*cost;} return {totalImages:fmt(totalImages,0), monthlyCost:dollar(monthlyCost), costPerImage:'$'+fmt(cost,4)};

faq.question: Which AI image generator is cheapest?
faq.answer: Stability AI's API is cheapest at ~$0.02/image. DALL-E 3 standard is $0.04/image. Midjourney's basic plan at $10/month is cheapest for moderate usage (up to 200 images).

formulas: Monthly Cost = Images per Day × Days × Cost per Image

tagline: Convert between LLM tokens and word counts
keywords: tokens to words, words to tokens, LLM token converter, GPT tokens to words
calcFn: var words,tokens; if(direction==='tokensToWords'){tokens=amount;words=Math.round(amount*0.75);}else{words=amount;tokens=Math.round(amount*1.33);} var pages=Math.round(words/250*10)/10; var readTime=Math.round(words/200); return {result:direction==='tokensToWords'?fmt(words,0)+' words':fmt(tokens,0)+' tokens', pages:fmt(pages,1)+' pages', readTime:readTime+' min read'};

faq.question: How many words is 4096 tokens?
faq.answer: Approximately 3,072 words (about 12 pages). The ratio is roughly 0.75 words per token for English text.

tagline: Compare GPU cloud rental costs for AI training and inference
keywords: GPU rental cost, cloud GPU pricing, A100 rental, H100 cost, AI training cost
calcFn: var rates={a100:1.50,h100:3.00,a10g:0.75,t4:0.35,l4:0.50}; var rate=rates[gpu]*gpuCount; var dailyCost=rate*hoursPerDay; var monthlyCost=dailyCost*days; var annualCost=monthlyCost*12; return {hourlyRate:dollar(rate)+'/hr', dailyCost:dollar(dailyCost), monthlyCost:dollar(monthlyCost), annualCost:dollar(annualCost)};

faq.question: How much does it cost to rent a GPU for AI?
faq.answer: Prices range from $0.35/hr for T4 GPUs to $3+/hr for H100s. Cloud providers like AWS, GCP, Lambda Labs, and RunPod offer various pricing tiers.

faq.question: H100 vs A100 which should I use?
faq.answer: H100s are 2-3x faster than A100s for training and inference. Use H100s for production training, A100s for development and fine-tuning, and T4/L4 for inference.

formulas: Monthly Cost = GPU Rate × GPU Count × Hours/Day × Days

tagline: Estimate costs for running autonomous AI agents
keywords: AI agent cost, autonomous agent pricing, LLM agent cost, AI workflow cost
calcFn: var pricing={haiku:{avg:0.75},sonnet:{avg:9},opus:{avg:45}}; var avgRate=pricing[model].avg; var tokPerTask=stepsPerTask*tokensPerStep; var costPerTask=(tokPerTask/1000000)*avgRate; var dailyCost=costPerTask*tasksPerDay; var monthlyCost=dailyCost*days; return {tokensPerTask:fmt(tokPerTask,0), costPerTask:'$'+fmt(costPerTask,4), dailyCost:dollar(dailyCost), monthlyCost:dollar(monthlyCost)};

faq.question: How much do AI agents cost to run?
faq.answer: A single agent task using Sonnet with 15 steps at 5K tokens/step costs roughly $0.67. At 50 tasks/day, that is about $33/day or $1,000/month.

faq.question: How to reduce AI agent costs?
faq.answer: Use Haiku for simple subtasks, cache repeated context, limit maximum steps, and use smaller models for routing and classification tasks.

formulas: Cost per Task = Steps × Tokens per Step × Average Token Rate

tagline: Calculate API throughput and rate limit requirements
keywords: API rate limit calculator, requests per second, API throughput, rate limiting
calcFn: var dailyRequests=dailyUsers*reqPerUser; var avgRps=dailyRequests/86400; var peakRps=avgRps*peakMultiplier; var concurrency=Math.ceil(peakRps*(avgLatency/1000)); return {dailyRequests:fmt(dailyRequests,0), avgRps:fmt(avgRps,1)+' req/sec', peakRps:fmt(peakRps,1)+' req/sec', concurrency:concurrency+' connections'};

faq.question: How do I calculate rate limits?
faq.answer: Divide daily requests by 86,400 seconds for average RPS. Multiply by 2-5x for peak traffic. Then ensure your infrastructure can handle peak concurrent connections.

formulas: Peak RPS = Average RPS × Peak Multiplier

tagline: Estimate vector database storage and query costs for RAG
keywords: vector database cost, Pinecone pricing, RAG cost, embedding cost, vector storage
calcFn: var chunksPerDoc=3; var totalChunks=documents*chunksPerDoc; var bytesPerVector=dimensions*4; var storageMB=(totalChunks*bytesPerVector)/(1024*1024); var embeddingTokens=totalChunks*avgChunkSize; var embeddingCost=(embeddingTokens/1000000)*0.02; var queryTokens=queriesPerDay*days*avgChunkSize; var queryCost=(queryTokens/1000000)*0.02; var hostingEst=storageMB>1000?79:storageMB>100?25:7; var totalMonthly=queryCost+hostingEst; return {totalChunks:fmt(totalChunks,0), storageMB:fmt(storageMB,1)+' MB', embeddingCost:dollar(embeddingCost)+' (one-time)', queryCost:dollar(queryCost), totalMonthly:dollar(totalMonthly)+' (incl. hosting est.)'};

faq.question: How much does a vector database cost?
faq.answer: Costs depend on data size and queries. Pinecone starts at $0 (free tier) to $70+/month for production. Self-hosted options like Qdrant or Chroma can be cheaper but require infrastructure management.

faq.question: What is RAG?
faq.answer: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) combines a vector database of your documents with an LLM. When a user asks a question, relevant documents are retrieved and added to the LLM prompt for accurate, grounded answers.

tagline: Calculate the return on investment from optimizing AI prompts
keywords: prompt engineering ROI, prompt optimization, LLM cost savings, AI efficiency
calcFn: var monthlySavings=monthlySpend*(tokenReduction/100); var engineeringCost=engineeringHours*hourlyRate; var paybackMonths=monthlySavings>0?engineeringCost/monthlySavings:999; var annualSavings=monthlySavings*12; var yearOneSavings=annualSavings-engineeringCost; var annualROI=engineeringCost>0?((yearOneSavings/engineeringCost)*100):0; return {monthlySavings:dollar(monthlySavings), engineeringCost:dollar(engineeringCost), paybackMonths:fmt(paybackMonths,1)+' months', annualROI:fmt(annualROI,0)+'%', yearOneSavings:dollar(yearOneSavings)};

faq.question: Is prompt engineering worth the investment?
faq.answer: Almost always yes. A 30% token reduction on $5K/month spend saves $18K/year. Even at $3K engineering investment, that is a 500% ROI. Quality improvements add additional value.

faq.question: How much can prompt engineering save?
faq.answer: Well-optimized prompts typically reduce token usage 20-40% while improving output quality. Techniques include few-shot examples, structured output formats, and prompt caching.

formulas: Monthly Savings = Current Spend × Token Reduction %
